title: GeoJSON
documentation:
## Purpose
GeoJSON (RFC 7946) is a JSON-based format for encoding geographic features — points, lines, polygons, and their properties — in a coordinate reference system (WGS 84 by default). The [GeoJSON](https://capdag.com/browse/media:geojson;record;textable) media type represents any `.geojson` file containing a valid GeoJSON object.

## When to use
Use this type when a node produces or consumes geographic vector data — for example, map layers, location datasets, geofencing boundaries, or route geometries. GeoJSON is the standard interchange format for web mapping tools such as Leaflet, Mapbox, and the Google Maps API.

## Notes
The top-level object is one of `FeatureCollection`, `Feature`, or a geometry type (`Point`, `LineString`, `Polygon`, etc.). The `;record;textable` tags indicate that the file is a structured record and human-readable text. Implementers should validate coordinate winding order (counterclockwise for exterior rings) as required by RFC 7946 and handle antimeridian-crossing geometries explicitly.
